example: The sagacious leader anticipated the crisis and prepared accordingly.
mnemonic: sag(감지하다, 라틴어 sagire) + acious = 잘 감지하는 → 현명한. sage(현자)와 같은 어원

example: Her effervescent personality brightened every room she entered.
mnemonic: ef(밖으로) + ferv(끓다, 라틴어 fervere) + escent = 끓어 넘치는 → 활기 넘치는. ferv(끓다): fervent, fervor

example: George Orwell's Animal Farm is a famous political allegory.
mnemonic: allo(다른) + agory(말하다, 그리스어 agoreuein) = 다른 것으로 말하다 → 우화, 풍유

example: The factory released noxious fumes that contaminated the surrounding area.
mnemonic: nox(해, 라틴어 noxa) + ious = 해로운 → 유독한. noc/nox(해): innocent, nocuous

example: The dulcet tones of the violin filled the concert hall.
mnemonic: dulc(달콤한, 라틴어 dulcis) + et = 달콤한 → 감미로운. dulc(달콤한): dulcimer

example: He has a proclivity for taking unnecessary risks in business.
mnemonic: pro(앞으로) + cliv(기울다, 라틴어 clivus=비탈) + ity = 앞으로 기울어지는 → 성향. cliv(기울다): decline

example: The meretricious decorations gave the room a tacky appearance.
mnemonic: meretric(매춘부의, 라틴어 meretrix) + ious = 매춘부처럼 겉만 꾸민 → 겉만 화려한

example: The despot ruled the country with an iron fist for thirty years.
mnemonic: 그리스어 despotes(주인, 가장). 절대적 주인 → 독재자. despotic(독재적인)

example: Several small streams coalesce to form a mighty river.
mnemonic: co(함께) + alesce(자라다, 라틴어 alescere) = 함께 자라다 → 합치다. al(자라다): adolescent(자라는 → 청소년)

example: She gazed out the window with a wistful expression, remembering her youth.
mnemonic: wist(알다, 생각하다, 고대 영어 witan) + ful = 과거를 생각하는 → 그리운

example: The preacher warned that sinners would face perdition.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + dit(주다, 라틴어 dare) + ion = 완전히 잃는 것 → 파멸. perdit(잃다): perdition

example: The professor provided a compendious overview of the entire course in one lecture.
mnemonic: com(함께) + pend(달다, 무게 재다) + ious = 함께 달아 모은 → 간결한. compendium(개요)의 형용사형

example: His polemical writing style provoked strong reactions from readers.
mnemonic: polemic(논쟁) + al = 논쟁적인. 그리스어 polemos(전쟁)에서 유래

example: The witness's testimony corroborated the victim's account of the incident.
mnemonic: cor(함께) + robor(강하게 하다, 라틴어 robur=힘) + ate = 함께 강하게 하다 → 확증하다. robor(강한): robust, corroboration

example: A surfeit of information can be just as confusing as a lack of it.
mnemonic: sur(위에, 초과) + feit(하다, 라틴어 facere) = 초과하여 하다 → 과잉. sur(초과): surplus, surpass

example: The divorce proceedings became increasingly acrimonious.
mnemonic: acri(날카로운, 라틴어 acer) + mony + ous = 날카롭고 쓴 → 신랄한. acr(날카로운): acrid, acrobat

example: The designer explored every permutation of the color scheme.
mnemonic: per(완전히) + mut(변하다, 라틴어 mutare) + ation = 완전히 바꿈 → 변환. mut(변하다): mutate, transmute, commute

example: The asteroid impact was a cataclysm that wiped out the dinosaurs.
mnemonic: cata(아래로) + clysm(씻다, 그리스어 klyzein) = 아래로 씻어내리다 → 대격변. cata(아래): catastrophe, catalogue

example: The novel portrays the moral depravity of the ruling class.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + prav(비뚤어진, 라틴어 pravus) + ity = 비뚤어진 상태 → 타락. deprave(타락시키다)

example: The opulent mansion featured marble floors and crystal chandeliers.
mnemonic: op(풍요, 라틴어 ops) + ulent = 풍요로 가득한 → 호화로운

example: The publisher abridged the novel for younger readers.
mnemonic: a(~으로) + bridge(다리) → 짧은 다리로 연결하다 → 축소하다, 요약하다. 어근 brev(짧은)와 관련

example: The ancient castle is part of the nation's cultural patrimony.
mnemonic: patri(아버지, 라틴어 pater) + mony = 아버지로부터의 것 → 세습 재산. patr(아버지): patriot, patriarch, paternal

example: The court adjudicated the dispute between the two companies.
mnemonic: ad(~쪽으로) + judic(판단하다) + ate = 판단을 내리다 → 판결하다. jud/jur(법): judge, judicial, jury

example: The company's predatory pricing strategy drove smaller competitors out of business.
mnemonic: predat(약탈하다, 라틴어 praedari) + ory = 약탈하는 → 약탈적인. praed(먹이): predator, prey

example: New DNA evidence exculpated the man who had been wrongly convicted.
mnemonic: '엑스컬페이트' → 'ex(밖으로)+culp(죄)+ate' 죄에서 빠져나와 무죄!

example: The old misanthrope lived alone and avoided all social contact.
mnemonic: mis(싫어하다, 그리스어 misein) + anthrop(인간) + e = 인간을 싫어하는 사람 → 인간 혐오자. anthrop(인간): anthropology, philanthropy

example: The fervid supporters cheered throughout the entire match.
mnemonic: ferv(끓다, 라틴어 fervere) + id = 끓어오르는 → 열렬한. ferv(끓다): fervent, fervor, effervescent

example: She has a predilection for Italian cuisine over all other food.
mnemonic: pre(미리) + dilect(좋아하다, 라틴어 diligere) + ion = 미리 좋아하는 → 편애. dilect(좋아하다): diligent, delight

example: The French Revolution overthrew the aristocracy.
mnemonic: aristo(최고의, 그리스어 aristos) + cracy(통치) = 최고 계급의 통치 → 귀족 정치. cracy(통치): democracy, bureaucracy

example: The artist was an iconoclast who rejected traditional painting styles.
mnemonic: '아이코노클래스트' → 'icon(우상)+clast(깨다)' 우상을 깨부수는 인습 타파!

example: Years of hardship had inured the soldiers to extreme conditions.
mnemonic: in(안에) + ure(작업, 사용) = 안에서 사용하며 단련 → 익숙하게 하다. use(사용): 반복 사용으로 적응

example: The dexterous surgeon performed the delicate operation with precision.
mnemonic: dexter(오른쪽, 라틴어) + ous = 오른손잡이처럼 능숙한 → 손재주가 있는. dexter(오른쪽): ambidextrous(양손잡이)

example: The diamonds coruscated under the bright lights of the exhibition hall.
mnemonic: corusc(번쩍이다, 라틴어 coruscare) + ate = 번쩍이다. 번개처럼 강렬하게 빛나는 것을 묘사

example: The cicatrix on his arm was a permanent reminder of the childhood accident.
mnemonic: 라틴어 cicatrix(상처 자국). 의학 용어로 상처가 아문 자리 → 흉터. cicatrize(흉터가 생기다)

example: Those who transgress the law must face the consequences.
mnemonic: trans(넘어) + gress(걷다, 가다) = 넘어서 가다 → 위반하다. gress(가다): progress, digress, regress, congress

example: The CEO's sagacity in predicting market trends made the company profitable.
mnemonic: sag(감지하다, 라틴어 sagire) + acity = 잘 감지하는 능력 → 현명함. sage(현자), sagacious(현명한)와 같은 어근

example: The criminal showed genuine contrition for the suffering he had caused.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + trit(갈다, 닳다) + ion = 마음이 완전히 갈리는 → 통회. trit(갈다): contrite, attrition, trite

example: Jesus taught many moral lessons through parables.
mnemonic: para(옆에) + bol(던지다, 그리스어 ballein) + e = 옆에 던져 비교하다 → 우화. bol(던지다): symbol, parabola, hyperbole

example: Only a few vestiges of the ancient civilization remain at the site.
mnemonic: vestig(발자국, 라틴어 vestigium) + e = 발자국 → 흔적. vestig(발자국): investigate(발자국을 추적하다)

example: The company offered refunds to placate the dissatisfied customers.
mnemonic: plac(기쁘게 하다, 라틴어 placare) + ate = 기쁘게 만들다 → 달래다. plac(기쁜): placid, complacent, please

example: The devious plan involved multiple layers of deception.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + vi(길, 라틴어 via) + ous = 길에서 벗어난 → 교활한. via(길): deviate, obvious, trivial

example: The malodorous waste from the factory polluted the surrounding area.
mnemonic: mal(나쁜) + odor(냄새) + ous = 나쁜 냄새의 → 악취가 나는. odor(냄새): odorous, deodorant

example: The vindictive neighbor deliberately damaged the garden fence.
mnemonic: vindic(보복, 라틴어 vindicta) + tive = 보복하려는 → 보복적인

example: He is an inveterate liar who cannot be trusted with anything.
mnemonic: in(안에) + veter(오래된) + ate = 안에 오래된 → 뿌리 깊은. veter(오래된): veteran

example: The king vouchsafed a rare audience to the foreign ambassador.
mnemonic: vouch(보증하다) + safe(안전한) = 안전을 보증하다 → 은혜를 베풀다. vouch(보증): voucher(보증서). 윗사람이 허락을 내려줄 때 사용

example: The adroit diplomat navigated the tense negotiations with great skill.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 a(~으로) + droit(오른쪽, 바른) = 바른 쪽으로 → 능숙한. 오른손잡이가 더 능숙하다는 관념에서

example: The heat of the afternoon induced a state of torpor in the animals.
mnemonic: torp(마비시키다, 라틴어 torpere) + or = 마비 상태 → 무기력. torp(마비): torpid, torpedo

example: The vainglorious general decorated his office with portraits of himself.
mnemonic: vain(헛된) + glorious(영광스러운) = 헛된 영광에 취한 → 허영심 많은

example: Writing in her journal provided a form of emotional catharsis.
mnemonic: 그리스어 katharsis(정화), kathairein(깨끗하게 하다). 감정의 정화 → 카타르시스

example: The ambassador interceded on behalf of the imprisoned journalist.
mnemonic: inter(사이에) + cede(가다, 라틴어 cedere) = 사이에 들어가다 → 중재하다. ced(가다): precede, concede, recede
